expected.basis: DevTools Elements panel, SauceDemo pre-auth login page. Searched all four detector selectors individually: #root PRESENT (one <div id="root"> in <body>, with the CRA <noscript> shell above it); #app, [ng-version], [data-reactroot] all ABSENT. Total = 1. TIMING: an earlier search on first load found no marker at all — #root appears only after the client script mounts, so this value holds on a SETTLED page, not necessarily at domcontentloaded. Reproduced in both incognito and a normal profile. Observed 2026-07-21.
DevTools Console (incognito, clean profile), SauceDemo pre-auth login page, settled: document.querySelectorAll('form').length === 1. Observed 2026-07-21.
DevTools Console (incognito, clean profile), SauceDemo pre-auth login page, settled: document.querySelectorAll('input[type="password"]').length === 1. Observed 2026-07-21.
DevTools Console (incognito, clean profile), SauceDemo pre-auth login page, settled: document.querySelectorAll('a[href]').length === 0 — the login page contains no anchors. Observed 2026-07-21.

notCovered: confidence / source / reason — derived grades have no ground truth; never assert them
authType is asserted against STATIC detection only (agent phase excluded) — see TD-166
_guidance: GUIDANCE for whoever fills or extends this fixture (metadata, not an assertion; this fixture is FILLED and human-attested). Each expected entry = { field, assert, value|values, basis }. field ∈ renderingModel|authType|crawlStrategy|appName|baseUrl|loginUrl|signals.<name>. assert ∈ equals|notEquals|oneOf|atLeast|atMost|present|absent. basis is REQUIRED (why the value is known). Signals available (names carry their definition, ADR-021 §2): signals.frameworkMountPointCount, signals.frameworkScriptCount, signals.rawDomAnchorCount, signals.formCount, signals.passwordFieldCount, signals.sameOriginNavigableLinkCount, signals.jsClickableCount. renderingModel (framework-rendered|static-rendered|unknown) is the observable rendering field (TD-163/ADR-021); appType is NOT an observable field — the platform is a structural fact of the execution context (ruling 2026-07-21), removed from the answer-key entirely.
